serving.natural_language_portion: One Cheesy Chicken Sausage OnStik from Old Chang Kee weighs approximately 80 g and is typically consumed as a single-serve snack on a stick.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken sausage, cooked — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 07072); adapted with cheese-filling adjustment using USDA Branded processed cheese data
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact HPB Singapore Foodo entry found for Cheesy Chicken Sausage OnStik. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy chicken sausage (NDB 07072, cooked) with upward adjustment to fat and energy to account for the processed cheese filling and deep-frying preparation typical of Old Chang Kee OnStik products. Sodium adjusted upward to reflect seasoning and cheese content. Values represent a best-estimate per-100g profile for this product category.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — sausage / processed meat analogous estimate; Atkinson FS, Brand-Miller JC, Foster-Powell K et al.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement found for Cheesy Chicken Sausage OnStik. GI of 28 is an analogous estimate based on processed sausage / meat-based snack entries in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021) international GI tables, which report GI values of 25–32 for processed meat sausage products. The low GI reflects the high protein and fat content with modest carbohydrate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (28 × 8.4) / 100 ≈ 2.4, where carbohydrate per 80 g serving = 8.4 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~680 mg per 100 g; ~544 mg per stick)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of Cheesy Chicken Sausage OnStik due to its high sodium content (~544 mg per stick), which represents approximately 24% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2,300 mg; frequent intake may contribute to elevated blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate with low GI, but high saturated fat and processed nature
type2_diabetes.guidance: The low glycemic index (~28) means Cheesy Chicken Sausage OnStik causes a modest postprandial glucose rise, but individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should be mindful of the high saturated fat content (7.2 g/100 g) and processed ingredients, which may adversely affect insulin sensitivity and cardiovascular risk when consumed regularly.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat, high sodium, and processed meat classification
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Cheesy Chicken Sausage OnStik is high in saturated fat (~7.2 g/100 g) and sodium (~680 mg/100 g), and is a processed meat product; individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should limit intake as regular consumption of processed meats is associated with increased cardiovascular risk per WHO and HPB Singapore dietary guidelines.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Cheesy Chicken Sausage OnStik is a popular Singaporean deep-fried snack sold by Old Chang Kee, a well-known local snack chain. It consists of a seasoned chicken sausage filled or topped with processed cheese, skewered on a stick, and deep-fried to a golden, crispy exterior. The texture is juicy and tender on the inside with a slightly crispy outer layer, and the flavour is savoury and mildly cheesy with a well-seasoned chicken base. It is typically sold as a single-serve grab-and-go snack priced around SGD 2.40. Nutritionally, it is a moderate-energy, high-fat, high-sodium processed meat snack providing approximately 265 kcal per 100 g, with 14.5 g protein, 18.5 g fat (of which 7.2 g is saturated), and 10.5 g carbohydrate. The sodium content is notably high at approximately 680 mg per 100 g, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at 28 (low), so it does not cause a sharp blood glucose spike, but the high saturated fat and processed meat classification make it unsuitable for frequent consumption by those managing Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular risk. It is halal-certified and widely available at Old Chang Kee outlets across Singapore.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Cheesy Chicken Sausage OnStik was found in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy chicken sausage data with adjustments for cheese filling and deep-frying. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for processed sausage products. Confidence is medium rather than low because the adaptation is grounded in approved primary sources and the food type (processed chicken sausage) is well-characterised nutritionally.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy chicken sausage (NDB 07072) with upward adjustments for cheese filling and deep-frying; no exact product-level data was available from HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA Branded Foods for this specific Old Chang Kee product. 2. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source data for this product category. 3. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source data for this product category. 4. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source data for this product category. 5. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0; confirmed zero as processed chicken sausage with cheese contains negligible vitamin C, consistent with USDA SR Legacy data. 6. GI of 28 is an analogous estimate based on processed sausage entries in the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement exists for this product. 7. Halal status is based on Old Chang Kee's publicly stated halal certification from MUIS (Majlis Ugama Islam Singapura). 8. Serving size of 80 g is an estimate based on typical Old Chang Kee OnStik product weight; exact weight may vary.
